title: Getting Started
text: ntrs provides S7-based interface standardizing neuropsychological test scores. typical workflow : Create score objects using factory functions (e.g., MOCATOTS(), ANIMALS()) Standardize scores std() batch-standardize data frame std_data()

title: Getting Started
text: neuropsychological test dedicated factory function wraps raw numeric scores metadata (valid range, error codes, etc.): see available tests:
code: moca <- MOCATOTS(c(25, 28, 30)) moca <ntrs::MOCATOTS> num [1:3] 25 28 30  @ label           : chr "MoCA"  @ domain          : chr "General Cognition"  @ short_descriptor: chr "MoCA Total Raw Score - uncorrected"  @ range           : num [1:2] 0 30  @ codes           : Named num [1:2] -4 88  .. - attr(*, "names")= chr [1:2] "Not available: UDS form submitted did not collect data in this way, or a skip pattern precludes response to this question" "Item(s) or whole test not administered" list_npsych_scores() [1] "ANIMALS"   "BOSTON"    "CDRGLOB"   "CDRSUM"    "CRAFTDRE"  "CRAFTDVR"  [7] "CRAFTURS"  "CRAFTVRS"  "DIGBACCT"  "DIGBACLS"  "DIGFORCT"  "DIGFORSL" [13] "DIGIB"     "DIGIBLEN"  "DIGIF"     "DIGIFLEN"  "LOGIMEM"   "MEMUNITS" [19] "MINTTOTS"  "MOCACLOCK" "MOCATOTS"  "MOCBTOTS"  "NACCGDS"   "NACCMMSE" [25] "OTRAILA"   "OTRAILB"   "OTRLARR"   "OTRLBRR"   "REY1REC"   "REY2REC" [31] "REY3REC"   "REY4REC"   "REY5REC"   "REY6REC"   "REYAREC"   "REYDLIST" [37] "REYDREC"   "REYFPOS"   "REYTCOR"   "REYTOTAL"  "TRAILA"    "TRAILALI" [43] "TRAILARR"  "TRAILB"    "TRAILBLI"  "TRAILBRR"  "UDSBENRS"  "UDSBENTC" [49] "UDSBENTD"  "UDSVERFC"  "UDSVERLC"  "UDSVERTN"  "VEG"       "WAIS"

title: Getting Started
text: standardizing, can check methods versions available given test: test registered defaults don’t need specify method every time: can changed globally set_std_defaults(): note changes default saved session session, may want set .Rprofile want persist.
code: list_std_methods(moca) [1] "norms"      "regression" list_method_versions(moca, "norms") [1] "nacc"    "updated" list_method_versions(moca, "regression") [1] "nacc"            "updated_2024.06" "updated_2025.06" get_std_defaults(moca) $method [1] "regression"  $version [1] "updated_2025.06" set_std_defaults(   moca,   method = "norms",   version = "nacc",   overwrite = TRUE ) ℹ Default method and version for "MOCATOTS" was previously set to "regression"   and "updated_2025.06". ℹ Will be overwritten. ✔ Set "nacc" as default for norms method on <MOCATOTS> get_std_defaults(moca) $method [1] "norms"  $version [1] "nacc"

title: Getting Started
text: Use std() convert raw scores z-scores. Covariates (age, sex, education, etc.) passed named arguments: override default method version, specify explicitly:
code: z <- std(moca, age = 72, sex = 1, educ = 16) z [1] -0.4166667  0.8333333  1.6666667 set_std_defaults(   moca,   method = "regression",   version = "updated_2025.06",   overwrite = TRUE ) ℹ Default method and version for "MOCATOTS" was previously set to "norms" and   "nacc". ℹ Will be overwritten. ✔ Set "updated_2025.06" as default for regression method on <MOCATOTS> z_reg <- std(moca, age = 72, sex = 1, educ = 16, race = 1) z_norms <- std(   moca,   method = "norms",   version = "updated",   age = 72,   sex = 1,   educ = 16 ) z_norms [1] -0.3532407  0.8313253  1.6210360

title: Getting Started
text: data frame multiple test score columns, std_data() standardizes : can override method/version per test class:
code: df <- data.frame(   age = c(72, 75, 68),   sex = c(1, 2, 1),   educ = c(16, 14, 12),   race = c(1, 2, 1),   moca = MOCATOTS(c(25, 28, 22)),   animals = ANIMALS(c(18, 20, 16)) )  result <- std_data(   df,   age = age,   sex = sex,   educ = educ,   race = race )  result age sex educ race moca animals     z_moca  z_animals 1  72   1   16    1   25      18 -0.3628727 -0.6295726 2  75   2   14    2   28      20  1.6463434  0.5252069 3  68   1   12    1   22      16 -1.1015874 -0.7205741 result2 <- std_data(   df,   methods = list(     MOCATOTS = c(method = "norms", version = "nacc")   ),   age = age,   sex = sex,   educ = educ,   race = race ) Warning: `race` is not needed to standardize using "norms" when version is "nacc" and will be ignored. result2 age sex educ race moca animals     z_moca  z_animals 1  72   1   16    1   25      18 -0.4166667 -0.6295726 2  75   2   14    2   28      20  0.8928571  0.5252069 3  68   1   12    1   22      16 -1.0303030 -0.7205741

title: Getting Started
text: prefer different default method session, use set_std_defaults():
code: set_std_defaults(   MOCATOTS(),   method = "norms",   version = "nacc",   overwrite = TRUE )  # Subsequent calls use the new default std(MOCATOTS(c(25, 28)), age = 72, sex = 1, educ = 16)

what: Calcaulate Functional Assessment Score summary — calc_FAS
title: Calcaulate Functional Assessment Score summary — calc_FAS
text: Calculates Functional Assessment Score summary (FAS) ten scores bills taxes shopping games stove meal prep events paying attention remembering appointments travel score scale 0 (Normal), 1 (difficulty, self), 2 (Requires assistance), 3 (dependent). , error codes 8 (applicable, e.g. never ) 9 (Unknown) also allowed, -4 indicates data collected. FAS calculated sum ten scores 8s 9s counted 0.

title: Calcaulate Functional Assessment Score summary — calc_FAS
code: calc_FAS(   BILLS,   TAXES,   SHOPPING,   GAMES,   STOVE,   MEALPREP,   EVENTS,   PAYATTN,   REMDATES,   TRAVEL )

title: Calcaulate Functional Assessment Score summary — calc_FAS
text: BILLS score ability write checks, pay bills, balancing check book. TAXES score ability assemble tax records, business affairs, papers. SHOPPING score shopping alone clothes, household necessities, groceries. GAMES score playing game skill bridge chess, working hobby STOVE score ability heat water, make cup coffee, turn stove MEALPREP score ability prepare balanced meal EVENTS score keeping track current events PAYATTN score ability pay attention understand TV program, book, magazine REMDATES score remembering appointments, family occasions, holidays, medications. TRAVEL score traveling neighborhood, driving, arranging take public transportation

Values can bare column names referencing columns data (e.g., age = age) explicit vectors (e.g., age = c(72, 65, 80)). methods optional named list keyed npsych_scores class name (column name). element named character vector form c(method = "...", version = "..."). elements optional within entry; omitted elements resolve registered default. NULL (default), npsych_scores columns processed.
